01_initial_request.input_data: NCBI database sequence for Caldicellulosiruptor morganii CE, protein structural model, FoldX software
01_initial_request.user_intent: To overcome poor thermostability and catalytic efficiency of Cellobiose 2-epimerase to enable efficient industrial biosynthesis of lactulose
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Achieve longer half-life at 65-75°C, higher kcat/Km values, and lactulose synthesis yield exceeding 35.9%

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Cellobiose 2-epimerase (CE) library for lactulose synthesis is scarce. [Gap] Need to identify a novel CE scaffold with potential for engineering. [Decision] To achieve novel enzyme discovery, chose to use NCBI BLAST to perform sequence mining on Caldicellulosiruptor morganii CE using CsCE as query, expecting to obtain candidate sequences.

parameters.selection_criteria: Unidentified AGE family epimerases/isomerases, lower identity to CsCE

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Identified CmCE (GenBank WP_045170066.1) with 70.8% identity to CsCE, selected for further study due to lactulose yield potential.

references.citation: Chen Q, He W, Yan X, et al. (2018)
references.relevance: Provides the CsCE query sequence context and reference for mining strategy.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] CmCE sequence is known but 3D structure is unavailable. [Gap] Structural model needed for rational design. [Decision] To achieve structural insight, chose to use Alphafold 2.0 to perform homology modeling on CmCE, expecting to obtain a 3D protein structure.

references.citation: Jumper J, Evans R, et al. (2021)
references.relevance: Supports the use of Alphafold for accurate protein structure prediction.

references.citation: Varadi M, Anyango S, et al. (2022)
references.relevance: Supports the use of Alphafold Protein Structure Database.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Substrate structure is required for docking analysis. [Gap] 3D conformation of lactose intermediate needed. [Decision] To achieve substrate modeling, chose to use Chem 3D ultra 12.0 to perform ligand structure generation on alpha-lactose intermediate I, expecting to obtain 3D ligand coordinates.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Generated 3D structure of the substrate intermediate.

references.citation: Wang L, Gu J, et al. (2021)
references.relevance: Identifies the intermediate I as the substrate during the limiting step of catalysis.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Protein and ligand files need preparation for docking. [Gap] Files must be formatted and grid box defined. [Decision] To achieve docking readiness, chose to use AutoDockTools to perform protein and ligand preparation on CmCE model and lactose intermediate, expecting to obtain prepared docking files.

parameters.preparation: Remove water molecules, add polar hydrogen atoms

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Prepared protein and ligand files for docking simulation.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Binding mode of substrate needs to be determined. [Gap] Active site interaction details unknown. [Decision] To achieve binding pose prediction, chose to use AutoDock Vina to perform molecular docking on CmCE and lactose intermediate, expecting to obtain lowest binding energy conformation.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Confirmed docking conformation for analysis of active site proximity.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Thermostability needs improvement via mutation. [Gap] Hot spots for stabilization unknown. [Decision] To achieve stability prediction, chose to use FoldX 4.0 to perform saturation mutagenesis calculation on CmCE, expecting to obtain DeltaDeltaG scores for all mutations.

parameters.filtering: Top 20 mutations with lowest DeltaDeltaG, exclude within 5 Angstrom of activity center

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Identified D226G with minimum DeltaDeltaG of -4.08 kcal/mol among selected mutations.

references.citation: Daudé D, Topham CM, et al. (2013)
references.relevance: Supports the use of FoldX for predicting stability changes upon mutation.
Supports the assessment of predictors of changes in protein stability.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Predicted mutations need experimental construction. [Gap] Mutant plasmids not yet created. [Decision] To achieve mutant library construction, chose to use PCR (Phanta Max) to perform site-directed mutagenesis on pET28b/CmCE, expecting to obtain 16 mutant constructs.

[Background] Predicted mutants need thermostability validation. [Gap] Actual stability performance unknown. [Decision] To achieve stability screening, chose to use Activity Assay to perform thermostability testing on mutant lysates, expecting to identify positive mutants.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: D226G retained 69.8% activity, identified as the only positive mutant among 16.

references.citation: Xu P, Ni ZF, et al. (2020)
references.relevance: Supports the method for improving thermostability through design.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Position 226 showed promise, saturation needed to confirm optimum. [Gap] Other amino acids at 226 not tested. [Decision] To achieve comprehensive site optimization, chose to use PCR to perform site-directed saturation mutagenesis at position 226, expecting to obtain all 226 variants.

references.citation: Jia DX, Wang F, et al. (2022)
references.relevance: Supports the strategy of saturation mutagenesis to ensure integrity of screening results.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Saturation mutants need stability comparison. [Gap] Best residue at 226 not confirmed. [Decision] To achieve optimum residue confirmation, chose to use Activity Assay to perform thermostability analysis on saturation mutants, expecting to validate D226G.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: D226G possessed the optimum stability performance among saturation mutants.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] D226G properties need detailed characterization. [Gap] Optimal conditions and half-life values unknown. [Decision] To achieve enzymatic profiling, chose to use Activity Assay to perform temperature, pH, and stability tests on purified D226G, expecting to obtain kinetic and stability parameters.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Optimal T 70°C, pH 7.5. t1/2 at 65°C increased to 149.7 min (vs 71.8 min for CmCE).

references.citation: Xu P, Ni ZF, et al. (2020)
references.relevance: Supports the fitting method for thermostability data.

references.citation: Chen A, Li Y, et al. (2015)
references.relevance: Supports the method for detecting residual activities.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Catalytic efficiency needs quantification. [Gap] Km and kcat values not determined. [Decision] To achieve kinetic analysis, chose to use Origin 2018 to perform Michaelis-Menten fitting on activity data, expecting to obtain kinetic parameters.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: D226G Km 60.2 mM, kcat/Km 1.27 mM-1 min-1 (higher than CmCE 1.01).

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Industrial application potential needs verification. [Gap] Lactulose yield not measured. [Decision] To achieve yield validation, chose to use HPLC to perform biotransformation analysis on lactose conversion, expecting to measure lactulose concentration.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Lactulose yield reached 45.5% for D226G, exceeding CmCE (35.9%).

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Mechanism of stability improvement needs structural explanation. [Gap] Dynamic behavior of mutant unknown. [Decision] To achieve mechanistic insight, chose to use YASARA to perform MD simulation on CmCE and D226G, expecting to obtain RMSD and RMSF values.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: D226G showed more stable backbone (RMSD) and rigidified alpha8/alpha9 loop (RMSF).

half_life_65C.interpretation: Increased from 71.8 min (CmCE), meeting goal for longer half-life at 65-75°C.

half_life_70C.interpretation: Increased from 52 min (CmCE), meeting goal for longer half-life at 65-75°C.

half_life_75C.interpretation: Increased from 33 min (CmCE), meeting goal for longer half-life at 65-75°C.

catalytic_efficiency.interpretation: Increased from 1.01 mM-1 min-1 (CmCE), meeting goal for higher kcat/Km.

lactulose_yield.interpretation: Exceeded 35.9% (CmCE) and surpassed the quantifiable goal of 35.9%.

03_success_verification.final_verdict: Achieved. The mutant D226G demonstrated significantly improved thermostability (higher half-lives at 65-75°C), enhanced catalytic efficiency (higher kcat/Km), and higher lactulose synthesis yield (45.5% > 35.9%), fulfilling all quantifiable goals.
